title: networking leak nsfs  mounts
body:
when using networking, we are leaking/not cleaning up nsfs mounts after the container stops.

for example, after running:
```
bin/podman --log-level=debug run -it --rm --network=bridge alpine ip a
```
a newly created mount of:
```
nsfs on /run/netns/cni-94a7ea7b-ebad-cff0-56d2-96d6be27db5c type nsfs (rw,seclabel)
```
remains after the container is done executing and removed.

comments.body: It remains after a `podman rm`? That seems pretty serious, as the routines to destroy namespaces should be firing then.
yes it remains ... i also sent you an email about several other things ... take a peek

Not sure what's going on, but this is happening in v4.2.0 on Ubuntu 22.04 and leaking thousands of these mounts, my machine takes forever to reboot, because of this.
Can we reopen this?
Please open a new issue. Almost guaranteed you have a different problem.
